name: fierce
title: fierce
summary: fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
description:
Domain DNS scanner
Fierce is a semi-lightweight scanner that helps locate non-contiguous
IP space and hostnames against specified domains. It's really meant as
a pre-cursor to nmap, unicornscan, nessus, nikto, etc, since all of
those require that you already know what IP space you are looking for.
This does not perform exploitation and does not scan the whole internet
indiscriminately. It is meant specifically to locate likely targets both
inside and outside a corporate network.
.
Because it uses DNS primarily you will often find mis-configured networks
that leak internal address space. That's especially useful in targeted malware.
Originally written by RSnake along with others at http://ha.ckers.org/.
This is simply a conversion to Python 3 to simplify and modernize the codebase.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use fierce because fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use fierce as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `fierce  [-h]  [--domain  DOMAIN]  [--connect]  [--wide]  [--traverse  NUMBER] [--search SEARCH` as documented upstream for fierce.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `fierce --domain google.com --subdomains accounts admin ads` as documented upstream for fierce.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `fierce --domain facebook.com --subdomains admin --traverse 10` as documented upstream for fierce.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `fierce --domain facebook.com --subdomains admin --search fb.com fb.net` as documented upstream for fierce.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair fierce with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `fierce --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install fierce. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `fierce --domain google.com --subdomains accounts admin ads`. Further invocations from upstream: `fierce --domain facebook.com --subdomains admin --traverse 10`; `fierce --domain facebook.com --subdomains admin --search fb.com fb.net`. fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: fierce - DNS scanner that helps locate non-contiguous IP space and hostnames against specified domains.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install fierce
